key_insight: An IoT-plus-AI construction management system can turn pipeline jobsite data into real-time safety warnings and parameter optimization for safer, cheaper oil and gas pipeline construction.
engineering_relevance: The approach is directly relevant to field engineering because it improves trench support decisions, welding quality oversight, and risk control across long, dispersed pipeline projects.
method: Built a cloud-edge multi-source perception system for on-site monitoring.
Used a lightweight visual detection model with depth-separable residual modules and a revised IoU-based loss.
Combined fuzzy comprehensive evaluation with improved particle swarm optimization to tune trench support parameters.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 71.6% average detection accuracy, 96.8% illegal-operation capture rate, 98.2% one-time welding pass rate, 4.2-minute hazard response time, and 28.6% cost savings after six months.
watch_item: The reported detection accuracy is moderate, so deployment quality may depend on site conditions, camera coverage, and reliable edge-cloud connectivity.
paper_title: Design and Implementation Strategy of Information Construction Management System Based on Internet of Things Technology and AI Algorithm in Oil and Gas Pipeline Construction
